verses.latin_vulgate: Primum quidem sermonem feci de omnibus, o Theophile, quæ cœpit Jesus facere et docere
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν μὲν πρῶτον λόγον ἐποιησάμην περὶ πάντων ὦ θεόφιλε ὧν ἤρξατο ὁ ἰησοῦς ποιεῖν τε καὶ διδάσκειν
verses.king_james: The former treatise have I made, O Theophilus, of all that Jesus began both to do and teach,

verses.latin_vulgate: usque in diem qua præcipiens Apostolis per Spiritum Sanctum, quos elegit, assumptus est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄχρι ἡς ἡμέρας ἐντειλάμενος τοῖς ἀποστόλοις διὰ πνεύματος ἁγίου οὓς ἐξελέξατο ἀνελήφθη
verses.king_james: Until the day in which he was taken up, after that he through the Holy Ghost had given commandments unto the apostles whom he had chosen:

verses.latin_vulgate: quibus et præbuit seipsum vivum post passionem suam in multis argumentis, per dies quadraginta apparens eis, et loquens de regno Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἷς καὶ παρέστησεν ἑαυτὸν ζῶντα μετὰ τὸ παθεῖν αὐτὸν ἐν πολλοῖς τεκμηρίοις δι᾽ ἡμερῶν τεσσαράκοντα ὀπτανόμενος αὐτοῖς καὶ λέγων τὰ περὶ τῆς βασιλείας τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: To whom also he shewed himself alive after his passion by many infallible proofs, being seen of them forty days, and speaking of the things pertaining to the kingdom of God:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et convescens, præcepit eis ab Jerosolymis ne discederent, sed exspectarent promissionem Patris, quam audistis (inquit) per os meum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συναλιζόμενος παρήγγειλεν αὐτοῖς ἀπὸ ἱεροσολύμων μὴ χωρίζεσθαι ἀλλὰ περιμένειν τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν τοῦ πατρὸς ἣν ἠκούσατέ μου
verses.king_james: And, being assembled together with *them*, commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which, *saith he*, ye have heard of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia Joannes quidem baptizavit aqua, vos autem baptizabimini Spiritu Sancto non post multos hos dies.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἰωάννης μὲν ἐβάπτισεν ὕδατι ὑμεῖς δὲ βαπτισθήσεσθε ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ οὐ μετὰ πολλὰς ταύτας ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur qui convenerant, interrogabant eum, dicentes: Domine, si in tempore hoc restitues regnum Israël?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ μὲν οὖν συνελθόντες ἐπἠρώτων αὐτὸν λέγοντες κύριε εἰ ἐν τῷ χρόνῳ τούτῳ ἀποκαθιστάνεις τὴν βασιλείαν τῷ ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: When they therefore were come together, they asked of him, saying, Lord, wilt thou at this time restore again the kingdom to Israel?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem eis: Non est vestrum nosse tempora vel momenta quæ Pater posuit in sua potestate:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτούς οὐχ ὑμῶν ἐστιν γνῶναι χρόνους ἢ καιροὺς οὓς ὁ πατὴρ ἔθετο ἐν τῇ ἰδίᾳ ἐξουσίᾳ
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed accipietis virtutem supervenientis Spiritus Sancti in vos, et eritis mihi testes in Jerusalem, et in omni Judæa, et Samaria, et usque ad ultimum terræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ λήψεσθε δύναμιν ἐπελθόντος τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος ἐφ᾽ ὑμᾶς καὶ ἔσεσθέ μοι μάρτυρες ἔν τε ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ ἐν πάσῃ τῇ ἰουδαίᾳ καὶ σαμαρείᾳ καὶ ἕως ἐσχάτου τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judaea, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum hæc dixisset, videntibus illis, elevatus est: et nubes suscepit eum ab oculis eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτα εἰπὼν βλεπόντων αὐτῶν ἐπήρθη καὶ νεφέλη ὑπέλαβεν αὐτὸν ἀπὸ τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And when he had spoken these things, while they beheld, he was taken up; and a cloud received him out of their sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque intuerentur in cælum euntem illum, ecce duo viri astiterunt juxta illos in vestibus albis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὡς ἀτενίζοντες ἦσαν εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν πορευομένου αὐτοῦ καὶ ἰδού, ἄνδρες δύο παρειστήκεισαν αὐτοῖς ἐν ἐσθήτι λευκῇ,
verses.king_james: And while they looked stedfastly toward heaven as he went up, behold, two men stood by them in white apparel;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui et dixerunt: Viri Galilæi, quid statis aspicientes in cælum? Hic Jesus, qui assumptus est a vobis in cælum, sic veniet quemadmodum vidistis eum euntem in cælum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἳ καὶ εἶπον, ἄνδρες γαλιλαῖοι τί ἑστήκατε ἐμβλέποντες εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν οὗτος ὁ ἰησοῦς ὁ ἀναληφθεὶς ἀφ᾽ ὑμῶν εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν οὕτως ἐλεύσεται ὃν τρόπον ἐθεάσασθε αὐτὸν πορευόμενον εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν
verses.king_james: Which also said, Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into heaven? this same Jesus, which is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc reversi sunt Jerosolymam a monte qui vocatur Oliveti, qui est juxta Jerusalem, sabbati habens iter.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ὑπέστρεψαν εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ ἀπὸ ὄρους τοῦ καλουμένου ἐλαιῶνος ὅ ἐστιν ἐγγὺς ἰερουσαλὴμ σαββάτου ἔχον ὁδόν
verses.king_james: Then returned they unto Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is from Jerusalem a sabbath day's journey.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum introissent in cœnaculum, ascenderunt ubi manebant Petrus, et Joannes, Jacobus, et Andreas, Philippus, et Thomas, Bartholomæus, et Matthæus, Jacobus Alphæi, et Simon Zelotes, et Judas Jacobi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε εἰσῆλθον ἀνέβησαν εἰς τὸ ὑπερῷον οὗ ἦσαν καταμένοντες ὅ τε πέτρος καὶ ἰάκωβος καὶ ἰωάννης καὶ ἀνδρέας φίλιππος καὶ θωμᾶς βαρθολομαῖος καὶ ματθαῖος, ἰάκωβος ἁλφαίου καὶ σίμων ὁ ζηλωτὴς καὶ ἰούδας ἰακώβου
verses.king_james: And when they were come in, they went up into an upper room, where abode both Peter, and James, and John, and Andrew, Philip, and Thomas, Bartholomew, and Matthew, James *the son* of Alphaeus, and Simon Zelotes, and Judas *the brother* of James.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hi omnes erant perseverantes unanimiter in oratione cum mulieribus, et Maria matre Jesu, et fratribus ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτοι πάντες ἦσαν προσκαρτεροῦντες ὁμοθυμαδὸν τῇ προσευχῇ καὶ τῇ δεήσει, σὺν γυναιξὶν καὶ μαριᾴ τῇ μητρὶ τοῦ ἰησοῦ καὶ σὺν τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: These all continued with one accord in prayer and supplication, with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: In diebus illis, exsurgens Petrus in medio fratrum, dixit (erat autem turba hominum simul, fere centum viginti):
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ταύταις ἀναστὰς πέτρος ἐν μέσῳ τῶν μαθητῶν εἶπεν ἦν τε ὄχλος ὀνομάτων ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτὸ ὡσ ἑκατὸν εἴκοσιν,
verses.king_james: And in those days Peter stood up in the midst of the disciples, and said, (the number of names together were about an hundred and twenty,)

verses.latin_vulgate: Viri fratres, oportet impleri Scripturam quam prædixit Spiritus Sanctus per os David de Juda, qui fuit dux eorum qui comprehenderunt Jesum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄνδρες ἀδελφοί ἔδει πληρωθῆναι τὴν γραφὴν ταύτην, ἣν προεῖπεν τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον διὰ στόματος δαβὶδ, περὶ ἰούδα τοῦ γενομένου ὁδηγοῦ τοῖς συλλαβοῦσιν τὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: Men *and* brethren, this scripture must needs have been fulfilled, which the Holy Ghost by the mouth of David spake before concerning Judas, which was guide to them that took Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui connumeratus erat in nobis, et sortitus est sortem ministerii hujus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι κατηριθμημένος ἦν σὺν ἡμῖν καὶ ἔλαχεν τὸν κλῆρον τῆς διακονίας ταύτης
verses.king_james: For he was numbered with us, and had obtained part of this ministry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hic quidem possedit agrum de mercede iniquitatis, et suspensus crepuit medius: et diffusa sunt omnia viscera ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος μὲν οὖν ἐκτήσατο χωρίον ἐκ τοῦ μισθοῦ τῆς ἀδικίας καὶ πρηνὴς γενόμενος ἐλάκησεν μέσος καὶ ἐξεχύθη πάντα τὰ σπλάγχνα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Now this man purchased a field with the reward of iniquity; and falling headlong, he burst asunder in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et notum factum est omnibus habitantibus Jerusalem, ita ut appellaretur ager ille, lingua eorum, Haceldama, hoc est, ager sanguinis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γνωστὸν ἐγένετο πάσιν τοῖς κατοικοῦσιν ἰερουσαλήμ ὥστε κληθῆναι τὸ χωρίον ἐκεῖνο τῇ ἰδίᾳ διαλέκτῳ αὐτῶν ἁκελδαμά, τουτέστιν χωρίον αἵματος
verses.king_james: And it was known unto all the dwellers at Jerusalem; insomuch as that field is called in their proper tongue, Aceldama, that is to say, The field of blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scriptum est enim in libro Psalmorum: Fiat commoratio eorum deserta, et non sit qui inhabitet in ea: et episcopatum ejus accipiat alter.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γέγραπται γὰρ ἐν βίβλῳ ψαλμῶν γενηθήτω ἡ ἔπαυλις αὐτοῦ ἔρημος καὶ μὴ ἔστω ὁ κατοικῶν ἐν αὐτῇ καί τὴν ἐπισκοπὴν αὐτοῦ λάβοι ἕτερος
verses.king_james: For it is written in the book of Psalms, Let his habitation be desolate, and let no man dwell therein: and his bishoprick let another take.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oportet ergo ex his viris qui nobiscum sunt congregati in omni tempore quo intravit et exivit inter nos Dominus Jesus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεῖ οὖν τῶν συνελθόντων ἡμῖν ἀνδρῶν ἐν παντὶ χρόνῳ ἐν ᾧ εἰσῆλθεν καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ἐφ᾽ ἡμᾶς ὁ κύριος ἰησοῦς
verses.king_james: Wherefore of these men which have companied with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,

verses.latin_vulgate: incipiens a baptismate Joannis usque in diem qua assumptus est a nobis, testem resurrectionis ejus nobiscum fieri unum ex istis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀρξάμενος ἀπὸ τοῦ βαπτίσματος ἰωάννου ἕως τῆς ἡμέρας ἡς ἀνελήφθη ἀφ᾽ ἡμῶν μάρτυρα τῆς ἀναστάσεως αὐτοῦ γενέσθαι σὺν ἡμῖν ἕνα τούτων
verses.king_james: Beginning from the baptism of John, unto that same day that he was taken up from us, must one be ordained to be a witness with us of his resurrection.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statuerunt duos, Joseph, qui vocabatur Barsabas, qui cognominatus est Justus, et Mathiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔστησαν δύο ἰωσὴφ τὸν καλούμενον βαρσαβᾶν ὃς ἐπεκλήθη ἰοῦστος καὶ ματθίαν
verses.king_james: And they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et orantes dixerunt: Tu Domine, qui corda nosti omnium, ostende quem elegeris ex his duobus unum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσευξάμενοι εἶπον, σὺ κύριε καρδιογνῶστα πάντων ἀνάδειξον ἐκ τούτων τῶν δύο ἕνα ὃν ἐξελέξω
verses.king_james: And they prayed, and said, Thou, Lord, which knowest the hearts of all *men*, shew whether of these two thou hast chosen,

verses.latin_vulgate: accipere locum ministerii hujus et apostolatus, de quo prævaricatus est Judas ut abiret in locum suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λαβεῖν τὸν κλῆρον τῆς διακονίας ταύτης καὶ ἀποστολῆς ἐξ ἡς παρέβη ἰούδας πορευθῆναι εἰς τὸν τόπον τὸν ἴδιον
verses.king_james: That he may take part of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas by transgression fell, that he might go to his own place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dederunt sortes eis, et cecidit sors super Mathiam: et annumeratus est cum undecim Apostolis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔδωκαν κλήρους αὐτῶν, καὶ ἔπεσεν ὁ κλῆρος ἐπὶ ματθίαν, καὶ συγκατεψηφίσθη μετὰ τῶν ἕνδεκα ἀποστόλων
verses.king_james: And they gave forth their lots; and the lot fell upon Matthias; and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.
